fault_hypothesis: The rate limit check on line 72 uses a strict greater-than comparison (`>`), but it should be greater-than-or-equal-to (`>=`) to deny requests when the number of recent requests is exactly at the limit.

constraints.intent: The comparison logic should deny access when usage is exactly at the limit, which is currently not the case.
The code path that allows a request should only be taken when usage is strictly below the limit.
It is invalid to report that a request is allowed while also reporting zero remaining requests.
